steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: An independent clause making a general claim precedes the blank. A series of independent clauses providing examples follows.

steps.title: Apply the rule for introducing elaborations
steps.content:
A colon introduces elaborating examples that follow an independent clause.

Choice D uses a colon to introduce the specific cases of different mycorrhizal associations.

The semicolon (A) would join the clauses equally rather than signal explanation; the comma (C) creates a comma splice; and no punctuation (B) produces a run-on.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
